channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Baltimore mayor fights back against attacks on DEI initiatives with ‘Definitely Earned It’ campaign
item.description: President Donald Trump’s attack on diversity, equity and inclusion programs is leading to pushback from Baltimore's mayor.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: One dead, 5 injured in 'active shooter' event at New Albany manufacturer
item.description: NEW ALBANY, Ohio (WCMH) -- One person is dead and five people are injured after a shooting at a New Albany warehouse Tuesday night. According to authorities, the shooting happened at the KDC/One building on the 8800 block of Smith Mill Road North at approximately 10:20 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Beachy, KRON4

item.title: Iran praises US for cutting foreign aid funding as it looks for a Trump message on nuclear talks
item.description: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — Iran's government seems to be welcoming some recent decisions by the United States — even though they happen to come from a man Iranian operatives have allegedly been plotting to assassinate.
item.dc:creator: NASSER KARIMI and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump to sign executive order barring transgender female athletes from competing
item.description: President Donald Trump will sign an executive order on Wednesday designed to prevent people who were biologically assigned male at birth from participating in women’s or girls' sporting events.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Russian, Uzbek nationals filed 132 fake voter applications in Pinellas County: DOJ
item.description: The 132 applications were submitted in envelopes with "identically formatted return and address labels," the complaint said.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, NewsNation

item.title: Dozens of artists contribute songs for L.A. fire benefit album available for 1 day only
item.description: Dozens of renowned indie artists are coming together to help the Los Angeles area recover from deadly wildfires by releasing a massive compilation album available for one day only. The new 90-track compilation album features artists such as R.E.M.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Earthquakes are rattling Greece's volcanic island of Santorini: What we know
item.description: Although Greece is no stranger to earthquakes, seismic activity of such frequency and intensity without having been preceded by a major quake is unusual, seismologists say.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What did US get from deals to pause tariffs on Canada and Mexico? Not that much, observers say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico — now on hold for a month — risked blowing up North America’s economy.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, MARÍA VERZA and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: District judge rules New Mexico schools are not required to follow 180 day rule
item.description: Under the state's public school code, the school year is supposed to contain at least 1,140 hours but there's no requirement for the number of days.
item.dc:creator: Bianca Hoops, NewsNation

item.title: Trayon White expelled from the DC Council for corruption but he's free to run for his old seat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The D.C. Council voted unanimously Tuesday to expel Councilmember Trayon White, who faces a federal trial on charges of taking tens of thousands of dollars in bribes to influence city contract decisions. White, 40, was arrested by the FBI last August.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump doubles down on floating Canada as 51st state amid tariff dispute
item.description: President Trump on Monday reiterated his desire to see Canada become part of the United States as he prepared to impose steep tariffs on Canadian goods.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Hundreds of ducks found sick or dead along Lake Michigan in H5N1 bird flu outbreak
item.description: The outbreak started in December and has now spread from a few reports in the suburbs to hundreds in the city, along Lake Michigan, over the last week.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: 5 things to know about Trump's plan to create a sovereign wealth fund
item.description: President Donald Trump is taking steps to overhaul the way the U.S. government invests its money and change the nature of America’s financial presence abroad.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KRON4

item.title: 5 takeaways on Musk, Trump war with USAID
item.description: President Donald Trump and billionaire tech entrepreneur Elon Musk have moved to shut down the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), which spent some $30 billion last year on humanitarian, development and security aid to more than 100 countries.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Major League Baseball fires ump amid gambling probe
item.description: Umpire Pat Hoberg was fired after it was revealed he shared betting accounts with a friend who bet on baseball.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gerber recalls all batches of Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks nationwide
item.description: Gerber Products Company has announced a nationwide recall and discontinuation of all batches of its Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks due to a potential choking hazard for infants and young children.
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man allegedly in country illegally accused of murdering elderly partner: police
item.description: Police in San Antonio have arrested a man who they say was in the country illegally and wanted in connection to the murder of his elderly partner.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Beloved park volunteer found dead after hike in Great Smoky Mountains
item.description: A hiker, who had been reported as missing, was found dead in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Matt Hollingsworth, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Some US businesses close in a 'day without immigrants.' But many say they can't lose income
item.description: Several businesses from day cares to grocery stores and hair salons closed Monday across the U.S. in a loosely organized day of protest against President Donald Trump's immigration policies.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO and MELISSA PEREZ WINDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas National Guard can now arrest and detain people who illegally enter the US
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas National Guard soldiers can arrest and detain people for entering the U.S. illegally from Mexico under an agreement with the Trump administration that expands the military's role in immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: MLB ump fired amid gambling probe
item.description: Major League Baseball has fired an umpire as part of an investigation into sports gambling. Umpire Pat Hoberg was fired after it was revealed he shared betting accounts with a friend who bet on baseball, according to ESPN reporter Jesse Rogers and later confirmed by the league.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: American bald eagles are having a moment, ecologically and culturally
item.description: WEST ORANGE, N.J. (AP) — Along the long road from American icon to endangered species and back again, the bald eagle — the national bird of the United States, often seen against a clear blue sky — is having a moment.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rory McIlroy overpowers Pebble Beach and wins in a runaway
item.description: The combination of one of golf's biggest stars and America's most scenic coastal golf course was the just the spark the PGA Tour needed, and both lived up to their reputations Sunday in the AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Airport employee injured in collision with aircraft wing at Chicago's O'Hare
item.description: The driver of a tug vehicle at Chicago O'Hare International Airport was injured Saturday evening after colliding with the wing of an airplane.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, ABC 4

item.title: Straka has late surge to regain Pebble Beach lead over Rory McIlroy and Shane Lowry
item.description: Sepp Straka watched his shot on the 10th sail over the cliff and onto the beach, starting a stretch of three straight bogeys. He rallied with four birdies over the final five holes to salvage a 2-under 70 and a one-shot lead over McIlroy and Shane Lowry.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China
item.description: "Tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico, and China are SIGNED!" White House spokesperson Harrison Fields posted on the social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Data from the deadliest U.S. air accident in a generation show conflicting altitude readings
item.description: Recovery crews and divers searched the Potomac River for remains and cleared wreckage Saturday from the midair collision of a passenger jet and Army helicopter that killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Minneapolis will pay $600,000 to settle with woman who says ex-Officer Chauvin knelt on her neck
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — The city of Minneapolis has agreed to pay $600,000 to settle a lawsuit by a woman who alleged that ex-Officer Derek Chauvin hauled her from her minivan and pinned her to the ground with his knee in January 2020, just as he did four months later when he killed George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CBS agrees to hand over '60 Minutes' Harris interview transcripts to FCC
item.description: CBS says it will turn over an unedited transcript of its October interview with Kamala Harris to the Federal Communications Commission, part of President Donald Trump's ongoing fight with the network over how it handled a story about his opponent.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says he ordered precision military strike against senior ISIS planner
item.description: President Trump said he ordered precision air strikes on Saturday morning targeting a "senior ISIS Attack planner" and others the unnamed individual recruited and led in Somalia.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: Hershey isn't sure why Kisses are called 'Kisses'
item.description: Why isn't it called a Hershey’s Smooch or a Hershey’s Cone? Even the people at Hershey aren’t exactly sure.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Opinion: Why Trump's meme coin is a cash grab
item.description: The coin – officially called $Trump – briefly ascended into the top 15 cryptocurrencies by market capitalization and attracted over a half-million buyers.
item.dc:creator: Maximilian Brichta Doctoral Student of Communication, University of Southern California, ABC 4

item.title: Colleges around the US cautiously navigate Trump's DEI crackdown
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — In Boston, Northeastern University renamed a program for underrepresented students, emphasizing “belonging” for all. In New Jersey, a session at Rutgers University catering to students from historically Black colleges had to be abruptly canceled. And around the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Deadly Eaton and Palisades fires 100% contained after 24 days
item.description: The deadly, wind-fueled Palisades and Eaton fires, which broke out 3½ weeks ago in Southern California, were declared fully contained Friday by the California Department of Forestry and Fire Protection.
item.dc:creator: Dennis Romero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Author's story of coping after a wildfire resonates with community affected by latest LA-area fires
item.description: PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — On a quiet summer evening in June 1990, Pico Iyer sat in his family home in Santa Barbara, California, when suddenly, he was surrounded by walls of flames five stories high.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A deadly flight out of Wichita has one of America's most historic aviation cities reeling
item.description: WICHITA, Kan. (AP) — When American Eagle Flight 5342 took off from Wichita, the Midwest city with a proud aviation history was glowing from a big moment. It had just hosted the next generation of Olympic hopefuls at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Flight recorders recovered in DCA crash
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) said Thursday that they recovered flight data recorders, known as black boxes, from the aircraft involved in the deadly mid-air collision near Reagan Washington National Airport earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, WGN 9
